reasoning: The event is asking whether the S&P 500 will drop by >5% in a single day between April 4, 2025 and December 31, 2025. The market traded at 7% probability on Nov 27, implying the event had not occurred between April and late November. Comprehensive web searches confirm that no such >5% daily drop has occurred between Nov 27 and today (Dec 7). As of Dec 5, the S&P 500 was approaching its all-time high, indicating a lack of extreme current market stress. With only about 16 trading days remaining in 2025, the historical base rate for a >5% drop within such a short window is exceedingly small (well under 1%), mostly representing a long-tail 'black swan' risk. Accordingly, I am lowering the probability from the late-November market anchor of 7% down to 3%, reflecting the passage of time and the absence of any ongoing market crash.
